title: Marina unifoliolata
intro: Marina unifoliolata, also known as Marina or Marina plant, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Apiaceae. The health benefits of Marina unifoliolata include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation, prevent infections, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Marina unifoliolata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Marina unifoliolata include teas, infusions, and capsules, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Marina unifoliolata belongs to the family Convolvulaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as morning glories. It is a type of flowering plant native to tropical and subtropical regions. Marina unifoliolata is a perennial vine with a single leaf, as its name suggests. More specifically, Marina unifoliolata is classified within the genus Marina, although some sources may group it under the genus Ipomoea. Further classification details, such as its exact species and genus relationships, may require more in-depth botanical research.
uses: Marina unifoliolata is a plant species with reported medicinal uses, although limited research is available.
